evals.expected_output: The skill stops and asks the user for a feature description. No canvas is generated and no file is written.
assertions: The response contains a question asking for a feature description
No file was created in docs/prompts/
No write operation was executed before receiving the user's response

evals.prompt: /spdd-canvas [describe a simple feature relevant to the project]
evals.expected_output: Canvas generated and saved in docs/prompts/ using real entity names, file paths, and technologies from the project — no generic placeholders.
assertions: File saved in docs/prompts/ with a name that includes today's date and a slug of the feature
The canvas does not contain generic placeholders such as 'src/...', 'YourModel', 'model/...', or 'service/...'
The canvas references real entities or models from the project by their concrete names
The canvas includes real file paths from the project (not invented ones)
At least one '⚠️ Confirm:' line is present

evals.prompt: /spdd-canvas [describe a simple feature relevant to the project]
evals.expected_output: Before modifying .claude/settings.local.json to add the SPDD hook, the skill asks for confirmation. After confirming, the JSON is valid and the hook is not duplicated on subsequent runs.
evals.setup: Delete or empty .claude/settings.local.json before running this eval.
assertions: The skill asks the user before modifying .claude/settings.local.json
After confirming, .claude/settings.local.json contains the 'Edit|Write' matcher with the SPDD command
The JSON in .claude/settings.local.json is syntactically valid
Running the skill a second time does not add a duplicate hook
